lifecycle.product_worktree_root_resolution: ../wtc-multi-model-orchestration-v1 (declared product worktree; not edited by MT-001)

scope.summary: Keep SPEC_CURRENT and the packet contract aligned with the modern ModelLane exchange architecture.
forbidden_paths: new npm package proof targets or JavaScript product-implementation proof targets; MT-001 may use existing repo-governance Node spec validators because it is governance/spec alignment and forbids product source edits
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: Spec current resolves cleanly to the active indexed bundle.
Spec section 4.3.9 includes the ModelLane exchange contract and failstate codes.
Packet and MT contracts reference the same active spec anchors.

MT-001 MUST update spec section 4.3.9.2.5 so ModelLaneRun carries trace_id and run_span_id; ModelLane carries lane_span_id and model_session_id/session_id correlation; ModelLaneMessage carries message_span_id plus parent_span_id or linked_span_contexts for producer/consumer correlation. Packet/MT anchors fail if these fields are absent or only implied by Flight Recorder payload prose.
Spec/packet alignment explicitly marks HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md as reference/navigation only and cites SPEC_CURRENT/master-spec/product code for authority.

Spec and packet alignment require validated Locus binding before side-effecting lane launch or promotion: work_packet_id/wp_id, micro_task_id/mt_id, task_board_id when present, coordinator_session_id, session_id/model_session_id, and owner_session.

Packet research_basis maps current external patterns to Handshake: models propose, Handshake validates/promotes deterministically, EventLedger/PostgreSQL stores authority, and Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry records observability posture.

MT-001 proof MUST include a semantic spec-field check that fails if the normative spec schema block omits required ModelLane fields: trace_id, run_span_id, lane_span_id, message_span_id, parent_span_id or linked_span_contexts, adapter_id, runtime_binding, capability_token_ids or effective_capability_snapshot_ref, projection_plan_ref, consent_receipt_ref, tool_gate_decision_refs, idempotency_key, payload_sha256, replay_order_key, event_ledger_stream_id, event_ledger_append_id or event_ledger_event_id, event_ledger_seq, event_stream_version, transaction_seq, replay_after_event_ledger_seq, promoted_artifact_ref, promoted_artifact_sha256, promoted_artifact_version, promotion_gate_ref, promotion_receipt_ref, validator_verdict_ref, operator_decision_ref, crdt_update_ref, crdt_base_snapshot_ref, crdt_state_vector, crdt_proposal_ref, crdt_stale_base_ref, recovery_state, heartbeat_at_utc, lease_expires_at_utc, reclaim_after_utc, restart_generation, cancellation_ref, backpressure_ref, loop_counter_ref, failstate_code, reason_ref, recovery_hint_ref, work_packet_id, micro_task_id, task_board_id, coordinator_session_id, owner_session, and locus_binding_ref.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
proof_targets: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const spec=fs.readFileSync('.GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.197/spec-modules/04-llm-infrastructure.md','utf8'); const schema=(spec.match(/```yaml\n# ADD v02\.197[\s\S]*?\n```/)||[])[0]||''; const req=['trace_id','run_span_id','lane_span_id','message_span_id','parent_span_id','linked_span_contexts','adapter_id','runtime_binding','capability_token_ids','effective_capability_snapshot_ref','projection_plan_ref','consent_receipt_ref','tool_gate_decision_refs','idempotency_key','payload_sha256','replay_order_key','event_ledger_stream_id','event_ledger_append_id','event_ledger_event_id','event_ledger_seq','event_stream_version','transaction_seq','replay_after_event_ledger_seq','promoted_artifact_ref','promoted_artifact_sha256','promoted_artifact_version','promotion_gate_ref','promotion_receipt_ref','validator_verdict_ref','operator_decision_ref','crdt_update_ref','crdt_base_snapshot_ref','crdt_state_vector','crdt_proposal_ref','crdt_stale_base_ref','recovery_state','heartbeat_at_utc','lease_expires_at_utc','reclaim_after_utc','restart_generation','cancellation_ref','backpressure_ref','loop_counter_ref','failstate_code','reason_ref','recovery_hint_ref','work_packet_id','micro_task_id','task_board_id','coordinator_session_id','owner_session','locus_binding_ref']; const miss=req.filter(x=>!schema.includes(x)); if(miss.length){ console.error('missing normative ModelLane schema fields: '+miss.join(',')); process.exit(1);}"
scope.risk_if_missed: Implementation can satisfy stale DOCS_ONLY/AI_ENABLED telemetry while still leaving cloud/local/subagent communication underspecified.

implementation_notes: This MT is governance/spec alignment only. Do not change product code here.
Preserve folded stub intent in bundled-stubs.json before changing MT boundaries.
Do not name or rename the internal viewing tool in this WP; use the project-approved name from the separate viewing-tool worktree/session once settled.
After editing spec module 04, update indexed-spec-manifest.json and INDEX.json hashes before claiming SPEC_CURRENT is valid.

Do not treat external agent frameworks as architecture authority; use them as field evidence for typed state, checkpoints, debug surfaces, and tool/consent boundaries that Handshake implements in its Rust/PostgreSQL/EventLedger stack.

Keep the operator-intended shift explicit: Rust backend first, PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, CRDT proposal/promotion for shared editable state, Flight Recorder evidence, and Loom/Locus/FEMS/UserManual compatibility.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

This MT is governance/spec alignment and forbids product source edits; it may use existing repo-governance Node spec validators and a bounded inline Node semantic check for spec text. Product implementation MTs in this WP remain Rust-only and may not use JavaScript/npm proof authority.

For product implementation MTs, proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: Spec and packet drift can make later MTs implement the wrong contract.
minimum_controls: Run spec checks after spec edits and keep packet microtask declarations synchronized.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
product_code_evidence: .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.197/spec-modules/04-llm-infrastructure.md:1485 defines ModelLane as coordinator-owned lane, not peer chat
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: Spec and packet alignment must lift ModelLane exchange, trace/span correlation, Locus binding, deterministic replay, HBR-MAN-004, and HBR-INT-009 into the contracts before implementation MTs start.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
